title: Rich Indian Cheese Samosas
description: A rich and comforting Indian dish featuring a cheese pull, made with dashi and deglaze-fond, and is diabetic-friendly, in the form of a samosa.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pan, heat the ghee over medium heat and add the cumin seeds.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is lightly browned.
Add the dashi powder and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the crumbled paneer cheese and cook until it is melted and well combined with the onion mixture.
In a large bowl, combine the flour and salt.
Add the water to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it is smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle and place a tablespoon of the cheese mixture in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a triangle and press the edges together to seal the samosa.
